title: Environmental factors predicting blood lead levels in pregnant women in the UK: the ALSPAC study.
ehr_evidence: Data were collected via self-completion postal questionnaires and whole blood samples.
summary: A study identifying factors contributing to maternal blood lead levels (BLL) in a large cohort of pregnant women in the UK.
key_findings: Higher educational qualification was a strong independent predictor of higher BLL.
Cigarette smoking, alcohol and coffee drinking, and heating the home with a coal fire were predictive factors for higher BLL.
Iron and calcium intake showed evidence of protective effects against higher BLL.
population: Pregnant women resident in the Avon area of the UK
